findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 39. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: The footer content (State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Resources, address, phone number) appears both in the HTML <header> area at the very top and again in the <footer> at the bottom. In the PDF, this footer text appears at the bottom of page 1 only (repeated on each page as a running footer). The HTML also places this text at the top of the document outside any landmark, which is a minor duplication but does not break reading flow significantly since it's in header/footer areas.
findings.evidence: Top of HTML body: 'State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Resources 1611 Mail service Center | Raleigh, North Carolina 27699-1611 919 707 9000' and again in <footer>: 'State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Resources 1611 Mail Service Center | Raleigh, North Carolina 27699-1611 919 707 9000'

findings.description: Minor capitalization difference in footer: PDF consistently uses 'Mail service Center' (lowercase 's' in 'service') while the HTML footer uses 'Mail Service Center' (uppercase 'S'). The HTML header area preserves the original lowercase 's'.
findings.evidence: PDF footer: '1611 Mail service Center'; HTML footer: '1611 Mail Service Center'; HTML header: '1611 Mail service Center'
